catalysts.catalyst_topic: Memory profit taking and Middle East tension causing stock declines
catalysts.catalyst_details: Asian memory stocks (Samsung, Hynix, Nanya Tech, Winbond, PSMC, Macronix) experienced significant declines (10-11%) overnight, attributed to unwinding due to Iran/Middle East tension and profit taking. Concerns about NAND cycle peaking with increased supply and waning consumer demand also contribute.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: However Asia this morning not shrugging it off and in TMT Asian memory rolled hard over overnight – Samsung -10%, Hynix -11%, Nanya Tech -10%, Winbond -10%, PSMC ()-10%, Macronix -10%, it feels like unwinding due to Iran/Middle East tension and PROFIT TAKING – Samsung is up 62% & Hynix 44% even after the fall as memory is one of the MOST Mo' exposed trade to date. We have also seen the debate around the cycle picking up, in particular for NAND, as people worry about peaking when supply kicks in and consumer demand wanes to a point where price & margin simply cannot sustain, adding to the broader worry of monetization/ funding/ credit risk of AI.

catalysts.catalyst_topic: Q1 2026 sales guidance below consensus
catalysts.catalyst_details: VAT is under pressure due to its Q1 2026 sales guidance (CHF240-260mn) being lower than consensus (CHF270mn), despite strong orders in Q4 2025 and a book-to-bill ratio expected substantially above 1. FY26 guidance expects overall growth.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: VAT under pressureas Q1 guide looks low and the key question for call given the orders seen in 4Q25, will be why is 1Q26 sales guidance relatively low, and what level of book to bill does this imply? While 1Q26 sales is guided at CHF240-260mn (vs consensus already above the top end at CHF270mn), but the book-to-bill ratio is expected to be substantially above 1 (consensus at 1.05x).

catalysts.catalyst_topic: Nvidia's $2bn investment in Lumentum and Coherent for advanced optics
catalysts.catalyst_details: Nvidia invested $2bn apiece in Lumentum and Coherent, including purchase agreements and access rights for advanced laser components. This supports R&D and expansion of US-based manufacturing for optics critical to AI data centers.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: DATA CENTER OPTICAL STOCKS ripped yesterday after Nvidia announced it will invest $2bn apiece in Lumentum (stock +7%) and Coherent (+13%). The investments will support R&D.

catalysts.catalyst_topic: Board restructure, CEO's increased focus on AI & R&D, CTO's planned departure
catalysts.catalyst_details: SAP is restructuring its board, with CEO Christian Klein shifting focus to A&I & R&D, delegating sales to Thomas Saueressig. CTO Muhammad Alam is set to depart in March 2027, raising questions about SAP's AI push.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: SAP board restructure – sees CEO Christian Klein focusing more on A&I & R&D. He is giving running sales that he took on in summer 2024 – when SAP overhauled the sales strategy. Sales will be run by Thomas Saueressig, (a board member who currently answers for after-sales matters and helping clients to deploy SAP’s software).Board member Muhammad Alam, who leads product and engineering, (seen as CTO and the technical guru on the board and came from MSFT) steps down at ends in March 2027 due to personal reasons – he is well regarded and do wonder if this is SAP admitting it needs to push hardier on AI.

catalysts.catalyst_topic: iPhone 17e and M4 iPad Air updates with effective price reductions
catalysts.catalyst_details: Apple announced the iPhone 17e and M4 iPad Airs, featuring stable starting prices for the iPhone 17e ($599 for 256GB) and reduced prices for higher storage tiers, which should support market share growth.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: Apple: Per GIR announced the iPhone 17e and M4 iPad Airs, where the most prominent surprises include (a) stable starting price on the iPhone 17e, and (b) 256 GB starting storage for iPhone 17e (v. 128 GB starting iPhone 16e storage). AAPL effectively reduced prices on the iPhone 17e through (a) holding starting iPhone 17e prices flat at $599 (256 GB iPhone 16e models started at $699 last year), and (b) 512 GB iPhone 17e now starting at $799 (v. $899 512 GB iPhone 16e starting price). We believe updates to iPhone 17e and M4 iPad Air should support AAPL's ability to continue growing market share across consumer electronics, particularly amidst industry-wide memory cost pressure.

catalysts.catalyst_topic: Expected decline in smartphone shipments and memory price hikes
catalysts.catalyst_details: Counterpoint predicts a 12.4% YoY decline in smartphone shipments in 2026 due to memory shortages, potentially extending into 2027. Korean companies have already previewed a 40% Q2 DRAM contract price hike.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: Mobile – last week IDC was IDC warned on mobile and now we Counterpoint saying that smartphone shipment to decline 12.4% YoY in 2026 impacted by memory shortage and the adjustment could last into 2027 while memory spot and contract price gap reaching 40-50% and likely to see contract price hike to narrow the gap while Korea companies already preview Q2 DRAM contract price hike by 40% while NVDA GTC will help drive AI memory momentum.

catalysts.company: TalkTalk, Virgin Media O2, Octopus Investments
catalysts.catalyst_topic: Capital injection and potential sale of business divisions to address financial challenges
catalysts.catalyst_details: TalkTalk's shareholders and lenders will inject £115 million, the third injection since December 2024, to support the indebted UK broadband provider as it explores selling parts of its business to potential buyers like Virgin Media O2 and private equity funds.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: TalkTalk's shareholders and lenders, including Ares Management, will inject an additional £115 million into the company to bolster its finances. This capital injection, the third since December 2024, aims to support the heavily indebted UK broadband provider as it seeks to sell parts of its business. Potential buyers are exploring acquisitions: Virgin Media O2 is considering the consumer division, while private equity funds and Octopus Investments are interested in the business unit (mmm we have heard this for a long time). TalkTalk has faced significant financial challenges, including payment delays to suppliers and a decline in its retail customer base from over 2.5 million in 2023 to 1.8 million. (Financial Times)

catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: Murtra (Telefónica) during his speech at MWC, advocates for European digital autonomy and the creation of a "European WhatsApp" for secure messaging. Murtra calls for market consolidation in the European telecom sector, urging regulators to be more permissive to foster larger, more competitive companies.

catalysts.catalyst_topic: Vodafone partners with Amazon Leo to expand 4G/5G coverage in remote areas
catalysts.catalyst_details: Vodafone has partnered with Amazon Leo to expand 4G and 5G mobile coverage in remote areas across Europe and Africa, aiming for easier/cheaper base station setup and enhanced network resilience. Rollout begins in Germany and other European countries, then Africa via Vodacom, with first mobile sites connected later this year.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: Vodafone has partnered with Amazon Leo to expand 4G and 5G mobile coverage in remote areas across Europe and Africa. (while Vodacom late 2025 signed a deal to use Starlink). The partnership aims to facilitate easier and cheaper setup of base stations, avoiding costly fiber or fixed wireless links. Amazon Leo's service will also enhance network resilience for emergency and critical online services by providing backup if fiber links are damaged. The rollout will begin in Germany and other European countries, followed by expansion across Africa via Vodacom, with the first mobile sites expected to be connected later this year. (Capacity)
